title: Chapter Thirty-Six: The Wine Cup
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Ma Se-in's expression changed the moment he saw Yeon-shin step onto the Martial Arts Training Ground.

Not anger this time. Not the arrogance of their first meeting. Something else had settled into the adopted heir's face -- something harder and more honest. His complexion was haggard, his courtesy rigid, and when he clasped his fists the gesture was precise.

"Would you match swords with me?"

"Your name?"

"Ma Se-in. We never exchanged them."

Yeon-shin studied his maternal grandfather's adopted grandson. They said the boy had been defeating white-level martial artists one after another in the Training Ground. Victories that should have swelled his pride. Instead, humility had settled over him like a cloak worn for the first time.

*A change in mindset. Interesting. Is this what famous families' self-reflection looks like?*

"I learned a lesson from our last encounter," Ma Se-in said. "I gained a great deal. Give me another chance."

Yeon-shin considered. He needed to complete the next mission without wasting time -- the blue martial uniform was the prize, and nothing could be squandered. Whether this boy could spark another inspiration was secondary. The Radiant Sword Style's framework demanded lightning in the mind, and divine thunder did not fall on command.

Still. The Desolate Ma Family's sword techniques were martial arts rarely seen even within the sect. Broadening knowledge was never wasted.

A breeze stirred his clothes.

"Isn't he a newcomer? Try crossing hands with me."

Cheong Myeong had materialised beside them without a sound. He patted Yeon-shin's shoulder and looked at Ma Se-in with his one visible eye. The black eyepatch over his left gave him a classical, slightly piratical charm.

Ma Se-in's face brightened. "Blue Eyes Demon Sword!"

"One Eye, now." Cheong Myeong smiled and turned to Yeon-shin. "Is it alright if I take this one?"

"Do you come to the Training Ground often?"

"If I don't, I'll fall behind. A sect full of monsters."

"Because of the eye?"

"Aye. Need to raise my senses back. A swordsman's vision halved -- that's no small thing."

He said it with a smile. Almost cheerful. Ma Se-in would make a fine training partner for rebuilding those instincts. Yeon-shin nodded and stepped back.

Do Yu-won murmured beside him as the two walked toward a corner of the training ground. "Strange. The rumours must be true. They say Blue Eyes Demon Sword treats people according to talent."

"Senior Cheong Myeong?"

"He is truly cold. Applies merciless standards to any martial artist who isn't a commoner. Both talent and character. He's been known to take heads from orthodox sect men who showed hypocrisy. That's why they call him Demon Sword."

"I can't tell at all."

"Because your talent is extraordinary. Other blue-levels avoid dealing with him. White-levels won't go near. The Desolate Ma heir picked wrong."

*He didn't treat Master Hyeon badly either.*

While Yeon-shin was mulling over this, the match was decided.

Ma Se-in had tried to exploit Cheong Myeong's blind left side. Clever. But blue-level martial prowess was a different world. Cheong Myeong had replaced sight with energy sensing -- his left palm struck without looking, catching Ma Se-in's solar plexus with an impact that sent the boy staggering.

Blood from his mouth. He collapsed.

Then the unexpected. Ma Se-in sat cross-legged where he fell, his lips curving upward. A look anyone in the martial world would recognise.

Enlightenment.

Orange-uniformed guards rushed in from all sides -- Desolate Ma Family retainers. Cheong Myeong stepped back with a smirk, returned to Yeon-shin.

"That friend. Not quite useless."

"This is Desolate Sect. Everyone is talented."

Cheong Myeong's lips twitched. He glanced at Do Yu-won, who bowed and retreated with the look of a man relieved to be leaving the demon swordsman's proximity.

*He humbled himself. He gained enlightenment.* Yeon-shin watched the kneeling boy surrounded by his guards. *Incredible. It shouldn't be possible for a person to change that fast.*

He revised his assessment. It was not the time to judge the level of inspiration.

He stepped onto the Training Ground.

---

"The sect leader wishes to commemorate your coming of age."

Yeon-shin had returned to the Radiant Demon Wing pavilion wearing the last light of sunset like an outer garment. Ma Jin delivered the words as though reporting a military order.

"You mean Desolate Sect will oversee the ceremony?"

"Aye."

Yeon-shin's brows drew together. The coming of age ceremony -- first of the four rites of life. Scholars held theirs at twenty. The martial world was different. The harsher the territory, the earlier they held the rites. Because death came young, and footprints should be left while there was ground to stand on.

It was not Yeon-shin's concern. For him, life itself was his footprint.

"When is the next mission?"

Ma Jin sighed. "The sect leader set this. She is acknowledging your talent."

*Then she should take a fruit from the World Tree and bestow it. Acknowledging talent while drawing such clear lines leaves little room for interpretation.*

Either the Desolate Sect Lord wanted him to reach the Divine Sword Squad hierarchy on his own merits, or her forces were sufficient and she felt no lack. Unknowable, for now.

"I don't want to waste the hours. In one hour I could comprehend dozens of sword technique variations. Please decline."

"She anticipated that response. The reward is wine whose properties overlap with no miraculous medicine in the world. Thunder Immortal Treasure Wine. One cup grants long life without illness."

The pressure at his crown pulsed. A faint heat, familiar and unwelcome.

Long life without illness, she said.

"I'll do it."

"Only one among those in the ceremony can drink it," Ma Jin continued. "Desolate Sect's ceremony elevates through martial competition. This time, white-levels from famous families are participating. Two or three years older."

"That won't be a problem."

He did not care about Ma Jin's flustered expression. *You never know. Whether it could slow the opening speed of the baihui acupoint even a little.*

---

The banquet hall was vast and luxurious, seats arranged along both sides.

Ma Yeon-jeok sat at the far end of the upper seats, his purple robe cascading over the armrest. Below him, Ma Jin's scar wrinkled as his father muttered.

"The sect leader interferes."

"Were you planning to hold it at the main family?"

"Not a matter of face."

The ceremony began. Boys and young men followed procedures that bore the shape of scholars' rites -- divination, hair binding, cap bestowal -- but the content was different. Martial artists' magnanimity stripped the solemnity away. The atmosphere was closer to a sparring hall than a temple.

Among twenty-five participants, Yeon-shin stood out. White clothes under a blue robe. Only three were white warriors. Most had grown up in Desolate Sect from the start.

The Desolate Sect Lord wore green ceremonial robes. She placed each cap with her own hands.

"Congratulations," she said when she reached him. Her red lips moved, and one corner rose. She stroked his cap once. "Lightning Flash will not take a courtesy name."

The spectators murmured agreement. Some martial artists who earned titles early took those names as their courtesy names. For Yeon-shin, Lightning Flash had already become the name he would carry for life.

"Now the last part," someone said.

"Wonder who will drink this year."

"Looking at achievements alone, no white-level can match Lightning."

"Even so, murim family potential is tremendous."

The Desolate Sect Lord tilted a jade bottle over a white cup.

*Thunder Immortal Treasure Wine.*

Life returned to his eyes.

Even meeting the Desolate Sect Lord's deep green gaze, his momentum held. She smiled and spoke.

"Celebratory wine for all of you. I will gift it to whoever takes it first. Try to claim it -- as you can."

Her clothes began to flutter. Green waves seemed to radiate from her body. Tremendous momentum pressed down on the hall.

It felt like being pulled into a deep swamp. Taking a single step became frightening. The Desolate Sect Lord had not even revealed a tenth of her true power.

*It wasn't this level last year, I'd wager.*

Those who hadn't yet become formal martial artists crumpled and fell. Remarkably, she caused no internal injuries. The seated guests were unaffected. Power control at the level of divine skill.

"Kuk!"

Someone groaned. Only three still stood -- Yeon-shin, Ma Se-in, and a young master from the Shin family.

Only one lifted his foot.

Yeon-shin felt the mysterious wind the Desolate Sect Lord emitted from her entire body. Streams of momentum swept over him, and something new thundered in his mind. Old techniques for breaking down energy barriers rose naturally from the depths of his knowledge.

This was communion.

Sky-blue light brushed past his eyes.

The first step was not Radiant Wing. He stepped forward, maximally conforming to the energy. A new footwork -- not the gap-creating First Step, but something else. Something that pierced.

Step.

Energy waves spread from where his foot fell. Colourless ripples expanding outward, gently pushing back the Desolate Sect Lord's green wind.

Radiant Wing Second Step. A footstep that pierced through momentum.

His cap flew off. Long black hair scattered wild. No one in the hall opened their mouth.

He climbed the platform as his hair settled strand by strand.

"You may savour it," the Desolate Sect Lord said, her mouth curving.

She stepped down. That was when Ma Se-in shouted.

"Supreme alone! All martial artists dream like you!" He knelt on one knee, bleeding, eyes bloodshot. "But the murim isn't like your thoughts! What do you think is the reason for creating sects, establishing families, founding alliances?"

Silence.

"I decided to uphold grandfather's will. I'll aim for the Desolate Ma Lord position. With the main family behind you, your position changes. Set a date and compete with me fairly. Defeat me for the heir position. It surpasses any wine. A position that commands a hundred swords is worth more than miraculous medicine."

Ma Yeon-jeok did not stop his adopted grandson. He simply stared at Yeon-shin's mouth.

*Such talk here? In public? They think I can't refuse if offered before a crowd?*

He looked at Ma Se-in. He looked at his maternal grandfather.

*I might have accepted once. If one family's prestige could obtain the World Tree's fruit. But it can't. And being bound to a family would cage my inspiration.*

He knew himself. The Desolate Sect Lord kept silent about his lifespan even to Ma Yeon-jeok. He would not beg for his life without compensation.

He opened his mouth.

"You spoke of the murim's principle. I know it well."

He stood alone on the platform. The blue robe. The youth looking down at the wine. A swordsman standing alone through the world's harsh waves.

He reached out. Lifted the cup.

"This is."

He brought it to his lips.

"The murim."

His words spread clear through the silent hall. Only the sound of the Thunder Immortal Treasure Wine flowing down his throat.

---
